role: Senior Fellow, Center for Peace and Security in the Middle East

recentTweets.text: Jolani and HTS, a listed terrorist organization, are also delusional if they think the world and Syria's neighbors will support his HTS-dominated "salvation government" as it imposes a transitional government on all of Syria that circumvents the UN-led 2254 political process.

I think Abu Muhammad al-Jolani must be delusional if he thinks that Syrians, having seen the departure of a 54-yr dictatorship yesterday, will sit still as he installs himself as another dictator in Assad's place.
https://t.co/UIFVkK8dMD

This is long-overdue justice for the U.S. soldiers Hizballah terrorist Daqduq ordered killed in Karbala in 2007. Our troops captured him only to have Pres Obama order his release to Nuri Maliki in 2011. Then he went back to Hizballah to attack Israelis for years. Good riddance.
People with no Iron Dome and no air force should not fire ballistic missiles at people with an Iron Dome and an air force.

Lebanon’s 2008 Doha Agreement, which cemented Hizballah/Iran’s political domination in Beirut, has been ended by Israeli airstrikes and ground brigades. 

For the first time in a generation, the Lebanese are about to have a real opportunity to determine their own future.
When Amos Yadlin says something like this, you have to sit up and take notice.
Whoever on the Israeli side designed the last ten days of operations against Hizballah is a genius.
https://t.co/AMoUE3cc4t
https://t.co/geiCPJLsrL
https://t.co/hGeglJrG4R
https://t.co/JMtbR0w9LG
https://t.co/FEqf7YRM6x
https://t.co/8yonJG0Q9A
https://t.co/Ch12Wxt3hi
LOKMAN SLIM
RAFIQ HARIRI
If Nasrallah’s death is confirmed, we will see an outpouring of joy across the Middle East like we haven’t seen in a long, long time.

People are learning in real time that Ivy league fellowships & "Foreign Affairs" articles don't translate into foreign policy acumen. Many lionized Biden's team in 2021. Now they've delivered 3 straight years of foreign policy failures. They don't understand how the world works.

Many in the U.S. are expressing shock over @POTUS Biden’s threat of an arms embargo on Israel. Personally, I’m not surprised. This is the logical conclusion of his policy toward Israel and Iran since he took office. It’s built on Obama’s policy of appeasing Tehran via the JCPOA.
If the Iranian regime can sanction UK government ministers, explain to me again why the UK government can’t sanction the IRGC?????

Last week the US House voted 389-32 for the “Assad Regime Anti-Normalization Act.” Huge condemnation of Assad.

Few noticed, however, that the bill explicitly kills the “Arab gas pipeline” idea the Biden admin championed. The bill makes any such deal involving Assad illegal.

1/5 https://t.co/z3ZzQwPgOj

In Baghdad no one dares speak the truth. The Iraqi PM and spokesmen live fearfully under the thumb of the thuggish Kata’ib Hizballah. They know very well KH/IRGC, not the Americans, are the biggest threat to themselves & the country. They employ Orwellian language to stay safe.

Too many otherwise rational people in DC  asserting that U.S. actions to stop the Iranian regime from killing Americans will put us on a slippery slope to Vietnam redux or WW3. Wrong. Those kinds of wars become far more likely if we don’t stop Tehran from killing Americans.

Mr. President @POTUS, you failed to prevent this from happening. Your policy of appeasing Tehran from your first week in office led the Supreme Leader to conclude he can attack Americans with impunity. You must hold the Iranian regime accountable, now. https://t.co/LNZ6nV5tId

If I were in the Biden administration’s shoes tonight, I would announce that I’m immediately starting a program to train Kurdistan Regional Government forces to operate the Patriot anti-missile system.
This cannot be allowed to go on. These are acts of war by the Supreme Leader and his regime.

If the US and its allies deter Khamenei and the IRGC from their unchecked (so far) aggression, every other regional conflict will become solvable:  Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, Yemen, and even the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

The Middle East crisis is dangerous but not complicated. It’s simply the result of the U.S & allied failure to deter Khamenei & his Revolutionary Guards. There’s no need to panic or to overcomplicate matters:  just do what’s necessary to restore deterrence.

By not enforcing oil sanctions, not responding forcefully to IRGC attacks on US troops, and issuing obtuse statements that disconnect the dots across the region, the Biden admin is creating the impression they intend to return to “de-escalation” talks with the Iranian regime

It also demonstrates that if the Israelis were to decide that Assad must pay the price for hosting Khamenei & Nasrallah’s northern front, the Israeli air force can take out any target (and anyone) in Damascus just as easily as they can take out the airport.

Repeated Israeli strikes to disable Damascus and Aleppo airports are, to my mind, a strong indication that 1) the Iranian regime is trying to move strategic weapons to or via Syria to open a northern front and 2) the Israelis are determined to preempt that.

Sorry, not believable. At no time in his 34-year rule has Ali Khamenei wanted a “broader conflict” with the United States. That’s why he uses proxies. And it’s why whenever the US retaliates strongly against him, he backs off.

It’s past time for senior US officials such as @secblinken to discard the fiction that the Iranian regime isn’t directing Hamas, Hizballah, Iraqi Hashd, & Houthis. They are. Any child can see it. Saying otherwise is nothing but a dishonest cover for a failed appeasement policy.

None of what is happening in the Middle East today had to happen. The US since 2021 has allowed the Iranian regime to run riot and the Iranians have deliberately set the region on fire. This is the absolutely predictable consequence of willful American negligence and weakness.

For USA & Europe, there will still be dangers, but the future looks bright compared to that of the China-Russia-Iran alliance. The latter’s client states Syria, North Korea, Belarus, Hizballah, etc have no future at all. They’ll collapse with their patrons. Good riddance.
11/11

Prediction:  the states of Russia, China, and Iran will each collapse within the next decade. Here’s why I think so:
🧵
1/11

Today the House of Representatives rejected, by a vote of 394 to 24, a proposal to lift US sanctions on Syria. The vote showed yet again that Congress will simply never relieve pressure on Assad. The Congressional pressure policy vs Assad is permanent. https://t.co/lIcPF9kbsA
This is the U.S. Congress’s response to the normalization of Assad. https://t.co/ArOuk3QTm2

The Arab capitals’ normalization of Assad without any preconditions is a policy that is doomed to fail, just as it did after he killed Rafiq Hariri. What we have to determine is what the real policy should be after normalization collapses. 1/
Qais Khazali had five US soldiers executed in Karbala in January 2007. Has the US statute of limitations expired for that crime?

Tehran won’t stop waging war on Saudi Arabia, regardless of any agreement, & China won’t stop Tehran from doing so. HOWEVER. The very fact of the Beijing-brokered talks is an enormous slap to US influence, one the Biden & Obama admins have brought about. https://t.co/aBQWigjp8P

Strong statement from @SenTedCruz on Brazil's outrageous decision to allow Iranian warships to dock at Rio--just 18 days after @POTUS hosted Lula in DC. Brazil is now enabling Tehran to threaten the Western Hemisphere on top of the threat they pose in the Middle East & Europe. https://t.co/i9NE2uNjmx

No coincidence Arab countries are normalizing with Assad just days after the Biden admin misguidedly weakened Syria sanctions. Thankfully, today the House will send a better signal via a bipartisan resolution opposing lifting sanctions on Assad & calling for a mechanism to stop

Looking at listings of earthquakes to hit Turkey and Syria, I can’t find something stronger than the 7.8 magnitude quake that just happened. And it was followed by a strong 6.8 aftershock. The 1999 quake that killed 17,000 was 7.4. This is very bad.

Two years ago today Hizballah took Lokman Slim's life because they feared his voice and words. 

To Hassan Nasrallah:  you can kill a man, but you can't kill a man's ideas. And you can't kill truth. Lokman foresaw your end--and there's nothing you can do about it. https://t.co/hB6yQh6ClC

Bombshell audio leaks coming out of Baghdad exposing corruption at Iraq’s Central Bank:  $8 BILLION diverted to Iran-backed militias, among other crimes. Acting CBI head Ali Al Allaq is coming to DC next week—US officials should demand answers for why Iraqi $ went to Iran. 1/4
It is impossible to pressure the Iranian regime to stop oppressing the Iranian people and stop destabilizing the region while simultaneously pursuing a sanctions relief deal with that same regime.
Calm down. A single drone attack against a single warehouse in Isfahan ≠ World War 3. Breathe.

Intelligence services everywhere should examine closely, and publicly explain, why they ALL vastly overestimated Russian military capability over the past 15 years. Because that overestimation has led to some very bad appeasement policies on several continents.

There are many urgent problems to confront in Syria. In my view, our top two concerns are 1) to compel Assad to stop waging war against the Syrian people & leave power; and 2) to totally roll back the IRGC. For all other issues, I’m open to suggestions as to order of priority.
Based on my experience with the Syrian conflict, I always assume every Russian cease fire announcement is just a ruse.

When the day comes that the Iranian people free themselves from the chains Ali Khamenei & his Islamic Republic have placed on them, the world is going to see a joyous volcanic eruption of Iranian creativity & culture that has been suppressed for so long. It will change the world.

The world should absorb the fact that:
- Assad can never win. He will never regain his strength.
- Syria will never be stable under his rule.
- The Syrian people will never stop opposing him. 
Time to implement policies recognizing this. Anything else is unrealistic.
6/6

So what should be done?

- Deprive Assad of $ to run his security state & crack down on dissent. Crush his Captagon revenues. Reinstate Caesar Act enforcement.
- Warn of consequences for crackdown. Expand prosecutions. Create international tribunal.
- Stop all normalization.
5/6

BUT-
- Destitute Syrians see Assad security/militias still enjoying fuel/resources.
- Also see Assad mafia's lives of luxury, dining at restaurants/clubs where 1 meal costs more than avg annual salary.
- Syrians also know Assad & mafia are making $ billions in Captagon trade.
3/6

THREAD:  

Assad's economy & state are collapsing:

- No fuel. 
- No electricity. 
- No commerce. 
- Streets empty.
- Assad just prints money to pay salaries/bills. Result: inflation making basic needs unaffordable.
- Pound crashed to 5,900 per $, making imports impossible.
1/6 https://t.co/dsvS1u0C62

Folks, pay attention to what is happening in Suwaydah in southern Syria. Big protests against the Assad regime in this Druze city; Assad forces fired on the crowd, killing a protester; the city is enraged. More to come.

Dangerous situation on Turkey-Syria border:

* Turkish airstrikes in Kobane & elsewhere in N. Syria

* Ankara retaliating for 13 Nov Istanbul bombing it says was done by Syria-based YPG

* With key US interests at stake, this crisis demands more USG attention than it’s getting. https://t.co/7uUpNaC5h4

Our indispensable NATO alliance with Turkey; our campaign against ISIS in Syria and Iraq; our cooperation with Turkey on Ukraine and other crises; our military presence in NE Syria and Iraq—all these US interests are at risk if this Turkey-PKK conflict can’t be resolved.

Few noticed, however, that the bill explicitly kills the “Arab gas pipeline” idea the Biden admin championed. The bill makes any such deal involving Assad illegal.

There are many urgent problems to confront in Syria. In my view, our top two concerns are 1) to compel Assad to stop waging war against the Syrian people & leave power; and 2) to totally roll back the IRGC. For all other issues, I’m open to suggestions as to order of priority.
Based on my experience with the Syrian conflict, I always assume every Russian cease fire announcement is just a ruse.

The world should absorb the fact that:
- Assad can never win. He will never regain his strength.
- Syria will never be stable under his rule.
- The Syrian people will never stop opposing him. 
Time to implement policies recognizing this. Anything else is unrealistic.
6/6

Folks, pay attention to what is happening in Suwaydah in southern Syria. Big protests against the Assad regime in this Druze city; Assad forces fired on the crowd, killing a protester; the city is enraged. More to come.

Dangerous situation on Turkey-Syria border:

* Turkish airstrikes in Kobane & elsewhere in N. Syria

* Ankara retaliating for 13 Nov Istanbul bombing it says was done by Syria-based YPG

* With key US interests at stake, this crisis demands more USG attention than it’s getting. https://t.co/7uUpNaC5h4

Our indispensable NATO alliance with Turkey; our campaign against ISIS in Syria and Iraq; our cooperation with Turkey on Ukraine and other crises; our military presence in NE Syria and Iraq—all these US interests are at risk if this Turkey-PKK conflict can’t be resolved.

WARNING: The Turkey-PKK conflict needs to move way up the USG’s priority list. The Biden admin must send an envoy to the region ASAP to deal with this crisis before it explodes again. Washington always prefers to ignore this conflict, but that approach isn’t sustainable anymore.

Putin's people claim Ukraine is preparing to attack itself with a dirty bomb so as to falsely blame Russia. This is the same game Moscow played whenever Assad used chemical weapons in Syria:  claim the Syrian opposition bombed themselves so as to implicate Assad.

Bashar al Assad's fate will be determined in Kherson and Crimea. If Vladimir Putin loses his war in Ukraine, eventually Bashar al Assad will lose his war in Syria. If Putin falls from power in Moscow, Assad will fall from power in Damascus.

For some, whatever the issue in Syria, their answer is always to stop pressuring Assad/Iran. In article after article they argue now is the time for the U.S. to exit & let Assad & Iran have their way. Slapping a "realism" label on defeatist appeasement doesn't make it realistic.

Attacking civilian targets to terrorize them and cause mass casualties is General Surovikin’s way of war. And it’s no mistake; it’s exactly what Putin hired Surovikin to do, just as the General did so many times in Syria.

Iraq is a big, strategically important country of about 45M people and GDP of almost $300B (per IMF). The US should’ve built a coherent policy to try to keep the country stable and out of IRGC hands. But Washington failed to do so, and now we see the consequences.
Eleven years is enough time to conclude that Assad cannot win the war he is prosecuting against the Syrian people.

Iranian state media today admitted & then quickly deleted the amazing admission that Iranian Amb to Yemen Irloo is same person as IRGC cdr Shahlai, who smuggled EFPs into Iraq, plotted with his American cousin to kill Saudi Amb Jubeir at Cafe Milano, & fired missiles at Riyadh.

Big escalation by the Iranian-backed militias in Baghdad over the past 48 hours as they directly challenge PM Kadhimi’s government by force. There’s no way this would be happening other than at the direction of Ismail Qaani and the IRGC-Quds Force.

Too much international silence on the situation in Deraa, Syria, where there are many reports the Assad regime aims to forcibly displace thousands of people who were under the protection of the Russians’ so-called reconciliation agreements. Forcible displacement is a war crime.

To the Afghan, Syrian, Lebanese, Iraqi, and Iranian people:  the world looks dark now, but this is not the end. What you are seeing from the US right now is not the real America, and it’s not what Americans want for us or for our friends. A change will come.

In announcing his absurdly implausible 95% election “victory,” Assad is simply reconfirming his own illegitimacy. No one in the U.S. is fooled. All the Syria experts in DC predicted a fake election, and the only surprise is that Assad decided to claim such a ridiculous number.

4 years ago, Bashar al-Assad attacked #Khan_Sheikhoun with sarin gas, killing 90+ Syrians. This grim milestone reminds the world why the Assad regime’s isolation must continue until there is full accountability for the atrocities the Syrian people have suffered at Assad’s hands.

During my tenure, I was able to witness all the crimes committed against the Syrian people. The evidence of the regime's crimes is overwhelming. I will always stand with the Syrian people. And I am confident that justice will be served.

Today is my last at the State Department. I’m honored to have served as Syria envoy and DAS for 2.5 years. I’m so proud of State’s Syria team, 100+ amazing professionals worldwide, all committed heart and soul to our Syria policy. I know they will continue the mission without me.

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 34 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Joel Rayburn. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Syria / Rojava, Iraq / Baghdad / Kirkuk, Oil / Energy, PKK.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 2 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 5 security-frame hits, 7 supportive or diplomatic hits, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 3 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2024-12-09T17:23:16Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

منذ أربع سنوات، قام بشار الأسد بقصف #خان_شيخون بغاز السارين وقتل 93 سوري. إن هذه الحقبة المريرة تذكر العالم كله بوجوب إستمرار عزل نظام الأسد حتى تتحقق المساءلة الكاملة عن كل الفظائع التي عانى منها الشعب السوري على يد الأسد.

4 years ago, Bashar al-Assad attacked #Khan_Sheikhoun with sarin gas, killing 90+ Syrians. This grim milestone reminds the world why the Assad regime’s isolation must continue until there is full accountability for the atrocities the Syrian people have suffered at Assad’s hands.

تعازيّ لعائلة وزملاء عزيزنا لقمان سليم. لقد كان قائدا شجاعا بأفكاره، ليس لمليشيات، وبالتالي لا يمكن هزيمته أبدا. أعداؤه لا يعرفون حتى الآن أنهم قد خسروا بالفعل. لن يستطيعوا إيقاف رؤيته بلبنان حر لجميع الأطفال اللبنانيين. ستعيش رؤيته طويلا بعد أن يتحول أعداؤه إلى تراب.

هنا من واشنطن، نرى وبوضوح أن نظام الأسد لن يستطيع التهرب من ضغوطات قانون قيصر ولن يستطيع تجاوز العزلة الدولية. 
رسالتي إلى النظام السوري في نهاية مهمتي: 
لم يعد لكم مكان تذهبون إليه، ليس لديكم خيارا سوى الخضوع ل 2254.

لقد وصل نظام الأسد لحدوده وما سيحصل الآن هو انهيار أكبر ضمن صفوفه، أسبوع وراء أسبوع وشهر وراء شهر. 
إذا تمكن الشعب السوري من البقاء متحـــــدا، حتما ستتاح له الفرصة لتشكيل مستقبله، ربما في وقت أقرب مما يعتقد الكثير من الناس.
العالم كله يرى أن نظام الأسد سقط من خلال سعيه دائما للحل العسكري بينما الحل الوحيد في سوريا هو سياسي يلتزم بقرارات الأمم المتحدة 2254 بما يتضمن انتقال سياسي في دمشق وليـــــــــــس هناك حل آخر.
خلال فترة ولايتي، أستطعت أن أشهد على كل الجرائم التي ارتكبت بحق الشعب السوري. لقد كانت الأدلة على جرائم النظام ساحقة. سأقف دوما مع الشعب السوري وأنا واثق من أن العدالة ستتحقق.

During my tenure, I was able to witness all the crimes committed against the Syrian people. The evidence of the regime's crimes is overwhelming. I will always stand with the Syrian people. And I am confident that justice will be served.

إلى كل السوريين: 
لن أتوقف عن العمل من أجل العدالة والسلام لكم ولن يتوقف زملائي في وزارة الخارجية. نحن كـلنــــــا متفقون أن مصالح وقيم الولايات المتحدة تدعو إلى نوع مختلف من الحكومة في دمشق، حكومة تعامل شعبها والعالم كله بشكل مختلف.

To all Syrians: I will never stop working for justice and peace for you. Nor will my @StateDept colleagues stop. We all agree: America’s interests and values BOTH call for a different kind of government in Damascus, one that treats its people and the world very differently.

هذا اليوم هو آخر يوم لي في وزارة الخارجية الأمريكية، تشرفت بالخدمة كمبعوث لسوريا ونائب لمساعد وزير الخارجية لمدة عامين ونصف. 
إني فخور جدا بأكثر من مئة شخص يعملون في ثلاثة قارات حول العالم وهم ملتزمون بقلبوهم وأرواحهم نحو تنفيذ سياسيتنا في سوريا وأعرف أن المهمة ستستمر بدوني أيضا.

Today is my last at the State Department. I’m honored to have served as Syria envoy and DAS for 2.5 years. I’m so proud of State’s Syria team, 100+ amazing professionals worldwide, all committed heart and soul to our Syria policy. I know they will continue the mission without me.
